items.title: Man joins brother and late mother's reconciliation journey after discovering he is Indigenous

children.content: Iain Seymour didn't realise he was Aboriginal until he discovered he was adopted and learned that his mother played a key role in a memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations.
Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

gallery.altText: Two men with arms on each other's shoulders looking at a fire

items.title: Modelling suggests 'second wave' of algal bloom in Spencer Gulf

children.content: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

image.altText: A man sits at a table near the beach with a laptop.

items.title: Original track designer steers clear of estimated $100m MotoGP remodel

children.content: Adelaide Street Circuit designer Bob Barnard estimates it could cost more than $100 million to remodel the track for the MotoGP, but the premier says that number has not been mentioned to him.

image.altText: A narrow bitumen road runs between trees and large patches of grass

items.title: Whyalla locals react to news of steelworks' final bidders

children.content: Whyalla locals welcome more certainty about the future ownership of the city's steelworks, but say it is still not enough to warrant business investment and workers coming back to the Spencer Gulf.

articles.title: Two remaining Whyalla steelworks bidders announced, BlueScope retains last offer right

children.content: Peter Malinauskas has announced M Resources and Jindal Steel are the two bidders remaining for the financially stricken Whyalla steelworks, while BlueScope Steel retains its right of last offer.

items.title: As the debate heats up, migrants are quietly keeping the bush running

children.content: Amid ongoing discussion about Australia's immigration intake and migration policies, regional residents say many industries in the bush cannot survive without overseas workers.

image.altText: workers from the pacific in red dirt field planting trees with tractor in front of them

items.title: Domestic violence operation results in 70 arrests in SA

children.content: SA Police say they targeted 351 offenders as part of Operation Storm Days of Action, attending 443 addresses across the state in April.

image.altText: Crime scene tape outside a home.

items.title: Murderer convicted of 1999 crime back in custody for breaching parole

children.content: Murderer Garry Thompson has been returned to custody for 12 months for breaching his parole conditions multiple times.

image.altText: The exterior of the Supreme Court of South Australia.

items.title: Outback school finally set to be rebuilt after mould forced demolition

children.content: More than two years since a health hazard led to Broken Hill's Willyama High School being knocked down, work on the "world-class" facility planned to replace is expected to begin.

image.altText: Five people in high-vis vests and hard hats with shovels of dirt at a construction site.

items.title: Heavy rain on Gus Lamont's family station prompts police to renew search

children.content: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.

image.altText: Gus Lamont

items.title: The extraordinary legacy of a trailblazing female driving instructor

children.content: One of South Australia's first female licensed driving instructors has been remembered for her tireless advocacy and devotion to her community.

image.altText: Black and white photo from newspaper showing woman speaking into a microphone

items.title: Police officer accused of rape denied he was 'sleazy', court told

children.content: A police officer goes on trial in South Australia's District Court after pleading not guilty to two counts of rape and one count of attempted rape.

image.altText: An old stone building with a red dome

items.title: Flights resume at training school after two killed in plane crash

children.content: An aviation school resumes flight operations at Adelaide's Parafield Airport four weeks after an instructor and a student onboard a twin-engine plane were killed when it crashed into a hangar.

articles.title: Israel kills new Hamas militant chief, along with his wife and children

articles.title: Israel warns all residents in southern Lebanon to leave

children.content: It is the first time Israel has made such a sweeping demand since US President Donald Trump announced in April a ceasefire had been imposed in Lebanon.

image.altText: A large smoke cloud billows from behind residential buildings.

articles.title: Iran's internet is back on. But it's not what it used to be

children.content: Reports of an increase in internet traffic are filtering through, but experts say Iran has a long way to go to get back up to speed.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's hands typing on a keyboard in a dark room.

items.title: Meet the Iranians who gather every night outside Khamanei's house

children.content: Outside the Tehran compound that was home to now-dead Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of his supporters gather every night to mourn the only leader many of them have ever known.

image.altText: A woman dressed in traditional clothing mourns outside on a street.

items.title: US Justice Department launches criminal probe into E Jean Carroll

children.content: The probe is focused on whether Carroll committed perjury in testimony tied to two civil lawsuits that she won against Mr Trump — one tied to ‌her allegations that he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and orange tinted glasses

items.title: ASX falls, government targets tech giants with tough scam laws

children.content: The Australian share market is trading lower while the federal government proposes tougher scam laws targeting tech giants. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Matthew Perry's assistant deserves to 'rot in prison', actor's friends say
